nodes.label: Decision-complete execution contract
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: The plan treats packets, binary evidence gates, WIP-1, do-or-park dates, and self-measurement as the mechanism for turning planning into shipped behavior.

nodes.label: Proactive capability ladder L0-L4
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Defines answers, reports, briefs, proposals, and learning as explicit maturity levels, with L2, L3, and L4 delivery targets.

nodes.label: ERA identity gap
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: The app calls itself proactive but has no composed, ranked, cross-module briefing, so the missing proactive heartbeat is an identity-level gap rather than an ordinary feature gap.

nodes.label: Unverified scheduler of record
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Five cron routes exist, but no scheduler configuration is committed and runtime liveness has not been proven.

nodes.label: Cron run ledger and liveness health
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Every scheduled job must record start and finish state and expose last-run health so time-triggered behavior cannot fail silently.

nodes.label: Deterministic-first briefing composer
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Verified typed signals and deterministic templates precede LLM phrasing because hallucinated due dates, quota cost, and latency would spend trust.

nodes.label: Proactive heartbeat spine
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: A single nervous system connects scheduler, run ledger, signal registry, composer, delivery policy, channels, and feedback; modules only contribute signals.

nodes.label: AI proposals over direct actions
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Proactive L3 attaches reviewable drafts, while money and schedule state remain human-confirmed and undoable.

nodes.label: Quiet-hours and push-budget delivery policy
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Default policy uses Beirut quiet hours, a three-push daily cap, severity classes, and digest overflow before proactive volume scales.

nodes.label: ERA router safety gap
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: The intent router sits upstream of money actions with zero tests and no confidence fail-safe, making fixtures and clarification-on-ambiguity prerequisites for new money intents.

nodes.label: HubPage monolith
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: HubPage is 5,798 LOC and growing; decomposition is allowed only when a feature rider pays for and verifies the extraction.

nodes.label: Fragmented conversation ownership
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Three conversation stores and two assistants exist without a declared owner-per-purpose or user-facing division of responsibility.

nodes.label: Shared AI context assembler extraction
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Moving context assembly out of the large AI route serves both tested chat behavior and the proactive composer, so one extraction pays twice.

nodes.label: Wake-word double dead end
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Porcupine is blocked by vendor registration policy and Azure Custom Keyword is unavailable in the browser JavaScript SDK; silent catch logic would have concealed the latter failure.

nodes.label: Timeboxed openWakeWord browser spike
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: openWakeWord with onnxruntime-web is the only free, accountless, browser-real path; it must meet measured kitchen accuracy or be formally parked after two sessions.

nodes.label: Foreground-only PWA wake ceiling
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Regardless of engine, a PWA can only listen while foregrounded with the screen on, bounding wake-word value to docked-phone scenarios until a native shell exists.

nodes.label: WIP-1 and binary gate governor
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: One packet in flight, dated gates, weekly cadence, and mandatory re-scoping after misses govern execution and prevent silent schedule drift.

nodes.label: ERA vital-sign metrics
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/ERA Awakening — Master Execution Plan (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Cron liveness, Speaks-First Ratio, briefing precision, notification regret, proactive hits, router safety, quota headroom, and capture share make the mission measurable.

nodes.label: Life-scale household operating system
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: The repository is described as a 795-file, 182-route, 32-module household OS spanning finance, schedule, kitchen, offline, voice, NFC, watch, guest, and AI surfaces.

nodes.label: AI-steering development operating system
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Feature Map routing, execution playbooks, enforcement hooks, and generational evidence audits form a second potentially shippable product: a system for steering AI engineering work.

nodes.label: Two-viewer household duality
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Sharing is architectural rather than bolted on: household links, visibility semantics, own-only escape hatches, and person-absolute colors preserve identity across both phones.

nodes.label: Immediate capture with Undo and drafts
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: The interaction model minimizes taps, acts immediately, offers Undo, and uses AI-generated drafts as the universal consent layer.

nodes.label: Lebanese reality layer
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: LBP-in-thousands, unreliable connectivity, real online probes, offline queues, and custom billing months encode the household's actual operating physics.

nodes.label: AnalysisReport reliability contract
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Strict structured output, precomputed inputs, tolerant validation, deterministic statistical fallback, and quota-aware model behavior constitute a production AI reliability pattern.

nodes.label: Physical-world state bridges
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: NFC, prerequisites, guest, watch, and voice turn physical events into task state-machine transitions, approaching consumer IoT workflow territory.

nodes.label: Generational evidence-stamped FABLED audits
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Scored, dated audits with delta ledgers and kill criteria preserve knowledge across sessions and model generations.

nodes.label: Meta-work gravity well
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Documentation delivers immediate safe wins while shipping risks failure, so knowing has outrun doing; execution incentives need WIP limits and hygiene rituals.

nodes.label: Junction safety-net inversion
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Tests cluster around already-strong pure money utilities while high-blast-radius junction routes and lifecycle side effects remain exposed.

nodes.label: Feature estate without a usage census
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Forty-plus modules create continuing entropy, yet weekly-active use is unmeasured, preventing evidence-based merge, park, or deletion decisions.

nodes.label: Unmeasured runtime AI economics
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Free-tier Gemini is load-bearing but daily usage and headroom are not measured, so quota failure appears as unexplained UX degradation.

nodes.label: Unproven second-user thesis
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: A system architected for two people is only half-proven until the partner has a workflow selected and operated as her own.

nodes.label: Household data gravity and disaster recovery
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Accumulating household truth is the moat and should be protected by exports, read-only integrity checks, and a documented restore path.

nodes.label: Household Event Spine
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: An append-only event choke point would supply briefings, timelines, audit trails, analytics, and automation mining; it is killed if top mutations produce too little genuine activity.

nodes.label: Household Ontology
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Lazy entity and alias resolution would connect merchants, inventory, recipe ingredients, and shopping lines into cross-module explanations.

nodes.label: Rituals engine
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Morning, weekly money, month-close, and trip-debrief experiences become scheduled guided conversations rather than isolated notifications.

nodes.label: Automation Miner
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Recurring household action sequences can become draft automation proposals, extending human-confirmed AI to behavior patterns.

nodes.label: What-If Simulator
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Interactive forecast scenarios let the household rehearse timing and spending changes, but only after the base forecast earns actual use.

nodes.label: Explainable money provenance
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLE — Final Consultation (2026-07-06).md
nodes.rationale: Every AI-spoken number should expose the exact source rows behind it, making auditability the basis for money trust.

nodes.label: Canonical household spend definition
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.rationale: One shared aggregation now aligns custom-month windows, debt returns, drafts, soft deletes, split children, hidden accounts, and partner-private totals across all money surfaces.

nodes.label: Server-side partner-private masking
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.rationale: Private rows count in household totals while descriptions, subcategories, and receipt URLs are redacted before reaching the non-owner; total derivability is an accepted trade-off.

nodes.label: Two-signal median-MAD outlier engine
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.rationale: Log-space category spikes, rare-large novelty, bimodal splitting, merchant cadence suppression, and graduation semantics reduce false positives while preserving meaningful anomalies.

nodes.label: AI budget proposal with deterministic floor
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.rationale: Outlier-cleaned history feeds a soft-clamped Gemini suggestion, but deterministic statistical output ensures a useful proposal still renders and manual allocation wins.

nodes.label: Household account visibility contract
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.rationale: Account reads are household-visible while expense-form selection is gated by is_public; partner and owner shortcut paths deliberately use different account scopes.

nodes.label: Persisted-query-cache staleness class
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.rationale: Correct server and RLS fixes can remain invisible across hard refreshes when a stable persisted cache and refetchOnMount false retain obsolete visibility-shaped results.

nodes.label: Template-driven NFC multi-transfer flow
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.rationale: Slugged transfer templates, modal chips, owner/partner account scoping, and auto-advance turn repeated NFC taps into a guided multi-transfer session.

nodes.label: Budget protection gap above pure utilities
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.rationale: Pure balance, recurrence, date, split, forecast, and anomaly suites exist, but route contracts and the canonical spend rules were initially unprotected; a July 4 delta added the first recurring route tests.

nodes.label: Production diagnostic route quartet
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.rationale: Analytics debug, Supabase debug, environment check, and Supabase check routes remain shipped, expanding a long-flagged hygiene issue into security posture debt.

nodes.label: Staged merchant intelligence
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.rationale: normalizeMerchant can power manual prefill, history autocomplete, merchant analytics, and eventually Kitchen price signals, with stage-one tap reduction as the stop criterion.

nodes.label: Money-to-Schedule bridge gap
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.rationale: Recurring dues, debt dates, and future purchases are still entered separately from Schedule and should wait for occurrence-engine unification before deep linkage.

nodes.label: Forecast surface gap
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.rationale: Tested statistical projection and anomaly cleaning already exist; the remaining gap is a daily account-balance series and two render surfaces.

nodes.label: Trip account creation bypass
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.rationale: Trips inserts accounts directly instead of using the canonical accounts API, duplicating visibility semantics after recent RLS and sharing changes.

nodes.label: Disconnected envelope funding flow
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.rationale: Salary-to-wallet transfers, balances, envelopes, and recurring commitment floors exist as parts but lack a semantic guided flow.

nodes.label: Canonical spend contract fixture
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.rationale: A purpose-built fixture should trip every spend and masking rule and assert that all consumers return the same number, preventing silent re-forks.

nodes.label: Money route contract test plan
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.rationale: Accounts, transactions, transfers, recurring payments, and allocations should pin validation, household expansion, conflict mapping, and posting semantics.

nodes.label: Persist-buster versioning rule
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.rationale: Any stable query result shape or visibility change must bump the persisted React Query cache version in the same commit.

nodes.label: Daily cashflow forecast engine
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.rationale: Recurring due expansion folded onto balances produces a daily projection for analytics and proactive warnings; existing tested substrates make this the cluster's low-risk 10x.

nodes.label: App-wide AnalysisReport adoption pattern
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.rationale: Document and reuse the structured-input, tolerant-validation, deterministic-fallback, ephemeral-render recipe across Schedule, Kitchen, and Trips without prematurely building a framework.

nodes.label: Recurring-shaped subscription auditor
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.rationale: Inverting merchant cadence suppression can detect recurring-shaped spend absent from recurring_payments and propose tracked subscriptions as drafts.

nodes.label: Sunday review and envelope-funding rituals
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.rationale: Weekly review and month-start salary-to-wallet-to-envelope choreography turn shipped analytics, transfers, suggestions, and recurring floors into felt habits.

nodes.label: Historical-rate LBP intelligence
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Budget/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.rationale: Historical FX stamping, purchasing-power drift, dual-currency net worth, and currency-aware imports exploit a locally distinctive data model.

nodes.label: Budget maturity score 5.4
nodes.rationale: Data correctness scores 8 and AI leverage 7, while test protection, bridges, and code health lower the overall score to 5.4.

nodes.label: Documented-rule enforcement drift
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/00 - Executive Summary.md
nodes.rationale: The architecture core is strong, but rules without lint, hooks, or tests repeatedly regress in logging, requests, invalidation, tests, and documentation.

nodes.label: Production console information exposure
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/01 - Security Vulnerabilities.md
nodes.rationale: Committed console output in sensitive server and client paths can reveal identifiers and internal state while drowning real incidents.

nodes.label: Unsafe raw request surface
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/01 - Security Vulnerabilities.md
nodes.rationale: Raw fetch is not uniformly wrong, but client mutations and long-running external calls need project-specific connectivity, timeout, and sanitized error semantics.

nodes.label: Multi-view cache invalidation risk
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/02 - Functional Vulnerabilities.md
nodes.rationale: Transactions, hub actions, shopping, item state, and debt settlement each affect several query families, while mutations may invalidate only their local family.

nodes.label: Utility-heavy test protection gap
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/03 - Structural Vulnerabilities.md
nodes.rationale: Tests mostly cover utilities and math, leaving route contracts, hook rollback/invalidation, offline replay, cron selection, and critical UI workflows exposed.

nodes.label: Shadow feature classification gap
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/03 - Structural Vulnerabilities.md
nodes.rationale: Atlas, blink, era, memories, reminders, today, and voice-conversation directories need explicit user-facing, internal, experimental, legacy, or folded ownership.

nodes.label: Repository-blind live RLS risk
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/01 - Security Vulnerabilities.md
nodes.rationale: schema.sql omits live policy and function bodies, so hot-table policy correctness and performance require direct Supabase verification.

nodes.label: Undo rollback-safety gap
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/02 - Functional Vulnerabilities.md
nodes.rationale: Failed undo operations may leave optimistic visibility, query state, and financial aggregates disagreeing unless prior snapshots are restored before errors are shown.

nodes.label: Timezone and recurrence edge risk
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/02 - Functional Vulnerabilities.md
nodes.rationale: DST, cross-zone viewers, cron parsing, and instant-versus-wall-clock intent remain silent-corruption domains requiring canonical helpers and boundary tests.

nodes.label: Invisible offline-queue overflow
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/02 - Functional Vulnerabilities.md
nodes.rationale: A bounded queue can reject actions during long offline periods without adequate visible warning, turning offline support into perceived data loss.

nodes.label: Mobile fixed-layout and overlay risk
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/02 - Functional Vulnerabilities.md
nodes.rationale: Header offsets, safe areas, sheet reachability, and floating-panel opacity are interaction invariants that require viewport verification rather than search alone.

nodes.label: Request-safety classification table
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/04 - Areas To Enhance.md
nodes.rationale: Classifying mutations, long calls, GETs, prefetches, probes, service workers, and server external calls prevents both unsafe exceptions and indiscriminate conversions.

nodes.label: Mutation-specific invalidation contracts
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/04 - Areas To Enhance.md
nodes.rationale: Each high-value mutation should document and test every query family that renders its changed state.

nodes.label: Risk-weighted behavior test ladder
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/04 - Areas To Enhance.md
nodes.rationale: Route contracts, mutation hooks, offline replay, timezone boundaries, and critical workflows are sequenced by confidence purchased rather than aggregate coverage.

nodes.label: Pinned hygiene and verification baselines
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.rationale: The living audit pins 594 console occurrences in 162 files, 240 raw fetch calls, about 99 mutation-adjacent calls, 93 tests with one failure, four debug routes, and two empty feature directories.

nodes.label: Red test suite as a meta-P0
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.rationale: A known-stale placement test makes every remediation verification red, either normalizing ignored failures or forcing repeated rediscovery, so it must be fixed before the audit loop.

nodes.label: Unrecoverable live-only Trip RPC logic
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.rationale: activate_trip and complete_trip exist only in the live database, so an incident could erase core product logic with no repository diff or restore source.

nodes.label: Dead code as agent-misdirection risk
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.rationale: Large zero-import files and obsolete voice modules have already redirected refactors, so their cost is functional misdirection in an AI-steered repository, not mere tidiness.

nodes.label: Feature Map drift correction
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.rationale: docs:check passes; the measured drift is in PM authority files and classification, not Feature Map-to-Feature Index alignment.

nodes.label: Campaign-owned remediation precedence
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.rationale: Audit findings should be promoted into the owning campaign checklist and struck through in the audit source ledger, then sequenced by irreversibility and verification prerequisites.

nodes.label: Metric ratchets and monthly audit deltas
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Codebase Audit 2026-07-01/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.rationale: Monthly reruns of pinned commands and CI ratchets that forbid count increases keep the audit alive without demanding an unrealistic big-bang cleanup.

nodes.label: Five-layer PM command center
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.rationale: The PM machine combines command-center files, campaign folders, generational deep dives, cross-cutting reviews, and tooling/enforcement.

nodes.label: Uniform module campaign structure
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.rationale: Six campaigns share feature state, vision, action plan, and checklist files, producing one mental model for evidence, severity, and implementation history.

nodes.label: Living FABLED 2 generation
nodes.rationale: FABLED 2 is the current evidence-scored delta layer while v1 remains frozen as historical baseline; code wins whenever the two disagree.

nodes.label: PM dashboard and enforcement tooling
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.rationale: Dashboard generation, an in-flight live markdown mutation server, tests, and hooks turn project management from static notes into partially executable software.

nodes.rationale: The face-intent-resolver-formatter seam is the extension point for Hub capabilities, but confident misrouting is especially dangerous for money intents.

nodes.label: Routing fixture table
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Hub & ERA/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: About forty real utterances mapped to expected face and intent are identified as the highest-value protection against confidently wrong actions.

nodes.label: AnalysisReport contract and fallback engine
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Hub & ERA/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Its tolerant schema, deterministic fallback, and reusable renderer are a shipped AI precedent that other domains are expected to copy.

nodes.label: HubPage monolith
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: HubPage reached 5,798 lines and grew by 292 lines in three weeks, so extraction is warranted when a shipping feature can ride on it.

nodes.label: useEraActions extraction
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Hub & ERA/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Extracting conversation-engine callbacks is justified as the substrate for an in-chat briefing card, not as speculative decomposition.

nodes.label: Shared AI context assembly
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Hub & ERA/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Moving context assembly to a tested library pays twice because reactive AI chat and the proactive composer need the same input seam.

nodes.label: Voice degradation-state contract
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Hub & ERA/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Token, SDK, worklet, and mid-stream failures need defined orb states and text fallback before voice polish.

nodes.label: Conversation-store ownership decision
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Hub & ERA/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Three conversation stores may legitimately serve different purposes, but each purpose needs one declared canonical owner before a fourth store appears.

nodes.label: Proactive household briefing composer
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Hub & ERA/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Per-module signals ranked into cards, push, and TTS are described as the product moat and the app's defining proactive behavior.

nodes.label: Reviewed proposal interaction pattern
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Hub & ERA/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Bulk-convert established a confirmable proposal grammar that can safely support multi-step money and automation flows.

nodes.label: Unified assistant capability registry
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Hub & ERA/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: ERA and the floating Budget assistant should route through shared capabilities so the same money question has one answer regardless of entry point.

nodes.label: Household daily activity log
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Hub & ERA/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A grouped read over existing messages, actions, and system events can create a family journal with no new write path.

nodes.label: Hub visibility policy and shopping reply receipts
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Private threads are excluded from immediate and cron push, while shopping child messages now have durable per-user unread receipts.

nodes.label: Hub and ERA maturity snapshot
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Overall maturity is scored 4.0: strong reactive chat and intent design, but test protection, proactive reach, and code health remain weak.

nodes.label: Kitchen loop-link state model
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Kitchen is evaluated as a set of closed or open loops; only four of nine links are built despite strong standalone tools.

nodes.label: Low-stock to shopping proposal keystone
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Detection and add paths already exist; the remaining wiring must be idempotent and present provenance-rich proposals rather than silently adding items.

nodes.label: Central stock-mutation invariants
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: One server-side stock helper should enforce non-negative stock, history rows, and threshold checks after every write.

nodes.label: Shared shopping add and dedupe path
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Manual and automatic adds should share pure idempotency logic outside the 3,181-line ShoppingListView and continue through existing APIs.

nodes.label: Schema-constrained recipe AI contract
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Saved HTML fixtures, tolerant validation, long timeouts, rate limits, and structured errors protect the currently untested recipe AI surface from prompt and model drift.

nodes.label: getMealsForRange read model
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A schema-free meal-plan projection lets Schedule and ERA consume the fact without creating a second owner or write path.

nodes.label: Ingredient-to-inventory matcher
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Matching is the hard reusable layer behind confirmed cooking deductions, pantry-aware recipes, and later restock behavior.

nodes.label: Kitchen price-observation layer
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Manual staple prices are the fastest seed; normalized statement grocery lines can later upgrade estimates without blocking an initial release.

nodes.label: Inventory has no standalone route
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Inventory is mounted inside Catalogue; documenting that as intentional would turn an ambiguous gap into an explicit product decision.

nodes.label: Kitchen domain-wide test gap
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Kitchen is the only campaign domain with zero tests; threshold boundary and scale-ratio tests are sub-hour prerequisites for safe loop closure.

nodes.label: Self-driving shopping list
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: The staged ladder is threshold proposal, check-off restock, then cadence learning only after enough real restock data exists.

nodes.label: Meals on planner and calendar surfaces
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Read-only meal rows in WebDayPlanner and calendar are identified as the cheapest visible cross-module Kitchen win.

nodes.label: Pantry analysis report
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: This is the proposed first non-Budget adopter of the AnalysisReport pattern and a test of whether the pattern is truly reusable.

nodes.label: Kitchen unit canonicalization service
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A single tested conversion service reduces quantity ambiguity in deduction, restock, matching, and price-per-unit calculations.

nodes.label: Physical shopping trip mode
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Store-order learning, large controls, budget running totals, and an expense draft close the physical-world shopping loop.

nodes.label: Kitchen briefing signals
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Kitchen/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Low stock, tonight's meal, expiry, and cost-versus-envelope become one module contribution to the household composer.

nodes.label: Kitchen maturity snapshot
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Overall maturity is scored 3.0: good standalone tools but weak loop closure, bridges, AI protection, and testing.

nodes.label: End-to-end notification pipeline
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Cron and in-app producers flow through the notifications store to push, bell, drawer, and alerts surfaces, then through click-routing handlers.

nodes.label: Unified notifications data model
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: The table already contains type, action, grouping, priority, severity, snooze, expiry, and domain links, so product behavior lags schema capability.

nodes.label: Hand-synchronized notification routers
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: In-app and service-worker maps can silently diverge because every type-to-destination change must be duplicated by hand.

nodes.label: Notification delivery policy engine
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Quiet hours, daily push budgets, priority classes, and per-type mute must gate every producer before proactive volume grows.

nodes.label: group_key notification grouping
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Producers already set group_key, so collapsing related rows is a rendering task rather than a schema project.

nodes.label: Notification action-outcome capture
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Act, dismiss, snooze, and click data must start accruing before timing or mute recommendations can ever be learned.

nodes.label: Notification learning loop
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: The proposed stages are engagement reporting, mute suggestions, then timing adaptation, with a volume-based stop criterion for a two-person household.

nodes.label: Notification Registry
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: registry.tsx became the TypeScript-side source of truth for routes, actions, icons, class, calendar sync, takeover eligibility, and retention; the service worker remains separate.

nodes.label: Unified realtime alerts page
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: The alerts page now shares the bell's data source, updates in realtime, groups by date and group_key, and offers system, scheduled, and unread filters.

nodes.label: Critical Alert Gate
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A globally mounted full-screen takeover provides a third attention layer for unacted high or urgent eligible alerts.

nodes.label: Google Calendar backup sync
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: One-way sync for scheduled items was wired into actual browser-side mutations, moved bookkeeping behind an access-gated admin client, and was live-verified for sync and delete.

nodes.label: Notification quick-action schema correction
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: The actions endpoint had been writing non-existent columns, so Done, Snooze, Confirm, and Dismiss silently failed until the column mapping was corrected.

nodes.label: Calm notification bell
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Finite new-arrival motion, severity-aware badge color, reduced-motion support, and a caught-up state replace perpetual urgency.

nodes.label: Explicit notification retention policy
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Actionable, summary, and hard-delete lifetimes need one documented rule aligned across producers and cleanup.

nodes.label: Notification cron logging debt
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: The unattended notification writers remain major console offenders and are the paths where structured Error Logs would have the most operational value.

nodes.label: Converged action inbox
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Drafts, reminder drafts, low-stock proposals, and briefing cards are all reviewable proposals with provenance and one-tap resolution.

nodes.label: Household-aware notification delivery
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Owners should get action pushes while partners get drawer awareness unless notify-all is explicitly requested.

nodes.label: Notification-to-conversation handoff
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A discuss action opens Hub with the alert as context so ERA can move, log, or otherwise resolve it instead of leaving a dead end.

nodes.label: Sunday notification digest
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Notifications & Alerts/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Below-urgent overflow from the push budget is composed into one weekly review notification.

nodes.label: Notifications maturity snapshot
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Overall maturity rose to about 5.8 after registry, alerts, action, critical-gate, and calendar work, while bell calm, drawer density, and delivery policy remain open.

nodes.label: WebDayPlanner and Plan My Day surface
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: The rebuilt reminders route uses a selected-day Focus planner plus a flexible-assignment tab, making it the natural host for cross-module projections.

nodes.label: Three divergent recurrence expansion engines
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Calendar, planner, and canonical library paths understand different exception dialects, so the same edited or paused occurrence can render differently by surface.

nodes.label: WebCalendar inline expansion engine
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: This untested path currently handles the fullest exception, pause, and reschedule dialect and therefore serves as the migration reference.

nodes.label: dayOccurrences expansion path
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: The tested day-level path powers reminders, Today, and Mission Control but only partially matches the complete exception and pause dialect.

nodes.label: Canonical expandOccurrences engine
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: The tested canonical design understands exceptions, pauses, and overrides but is wired to no live surface.

nodes.label: Dual occurrence-move dialects
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Live UI writes postponed_to actions while the canonical engine reads rescheduled_to exceptions; a translator is the first unification step.

nodes.label: Idempotent occurrence action upserts
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Complete, postpone, cancel, and skip use a natural conflict key so double taps and offline replays do not duplicate or loop on 500 errors.

nodes.label: Schedule RPC and RLS schema recovery
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: schema.sql now includes get_schedule_bundle and thirteen policies, proving live function bodies can be recovered into reviewable repository truth.

nodes.label: Stale source-regex placement guard
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A known stale source-text assertion leaves the suite permanently red, making genuine regressions indistinguishable from accepted noise.

nodes.label: Behavioral flexible-placement guard
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Testing the actual expansion output preserves the rule across helper extraction and restores a trustworthy green suite.

nodes.label: Dead MobileItemForm
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: The 1,363-line file has zero importers and already caused a misdirected refactor, illustrating the absence of a routine hygiene execution slot.

nodes.label: Surface-by-surface recurrence engine migration
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Move dialects are unified first, then Today, planner, and calendar migrate independently with fixture-diff tests and no big bang.

nodes.label: Single occurrence action sheet
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: One component keeps Complete, Skip, Move, edit-scope, and reassignment verbs consistent after expansion semantics converge.

nodes.label: getWeekShape schedule read model
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Per-person load, overdue streaks, recurring pauses, free evenings, and meal slots form the highest-leverage outward schedule function.

nodes.label: time_window prerequisite evaluator
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A small implemented evaluator with one test would prove the dormant-to-pending prerequisite path before expanding the evaluator program.

nodes.label: Arrive-home prerequisite trigger
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: An NFC door event can activate items already captured with home context, but only if NFC taps become an actual household habit.

nodes.label: Stored day_plans intent
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Rest or productivity intent is captured but consumed by no optimizer or presentation logic.

nodes.label: Intent-aware day planning
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A deterministic ranking function can protect rest days and pull overdue work into productivity days without schema changes or AI.

nodes.label: Schedule pressure index
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A per-person daily load score could power planner warnings and household fairness, with raw counts as fallback if weights do not feel credible.

nodes.label: Paused-by-trip transparency rows
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Ghost rows make masked occurrences visible and double as a visual verifier for Trips cascade correctness.

nodes.label: Partner-private responsible-me bundle blind spot
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Schedule/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: The shipped bundle can omit partner-private items assigned to the current user; expanding assignment entry points makes this increasingly unsafe to leave parked.

nodes.label: Schedule maturity snapshot
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Overall maturity is scored 5.3: household semantics are strong, while engine correctness, code health, and outward bridges remain constrained.

nodes.label: Trip side-effect reversal ledger
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Every trip cascade mutation is intended to be logged so completion can reverse it, which is strong architecture if verified.

nodes.label: Unverified trip activate-complete cascades
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Household and solo round trips, ledger symmetry, payment exclusions, manual reassignment, and account visibility have never been deliberately exercised end to end.

nodes.label: Missing activate_trip and complete_trip source
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: The core lifecycle function bodies have zero repository matches, leaving cascade logic unrecoverable and changes unreviewable.

nodes.label: Trip-written recurrence pauses
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Trip activation writes recurrence_pauses that Schedule expansion engines must interpret and completion must reverse.

nodes.label: Trips environmental drift risk
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/1 - FABLED 2 — Current Implementation.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Trips is frozen while accounts semantics, occurrence behavior, engine architecture, and reassignment controls around it changed.

nodes.label: Trip account-creation copy drift
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/2 - FABLED 2 — Gaps & Missing.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Trips copied account creation before Budget changed visibility semantics and RLS, so the two paths now definitely differ in assumptions.

nodes.label: Trip RPC snapshot recovery plan
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Exporting pg_get_functiondef into a dated migration and schema.sql converts hidden live logic into recoverable, reviewable source.

nodes.label: Trip cascade round-trip verification session
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A throwaway household and solo trip with representative child records can verify every ledger effect and reversal before dependent features are built.

nodes.label: Trip ledger-symmetry assertion
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A saved SQL assertion is the module's practical regression test until real DB test infrastructure exists.

nodes.label: Explicit Trips freeze decision
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/3 - FABLED 2 — Optimization Plan.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: If recovery and verification are not booked, naming a trigger-based freeze avoids the recurring tax and false status of rolling deferral.

nodes.label: Trip impact and restoration panel
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A read-only ledger summary shows what activation changed and what completion will restore, becoming both trust UI and a permanent verification surface.

nodes.label: ERA trip re-entry briefing
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Trips, ledger effects, and trip-account spend already contain the ingredients for a cheap high-value composer signal.

nodes.label: Trip spending rollup
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Spend-versus-budget and post-trip summaries should reuse the canonical spending definition rather than create another counting dialect.

nodes.label: Generalized household modes engine
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Trip activation and reversal could generalize to sick, guest, and crunch modes only after real-trip usage proves the ledger abstraction.

nodes.label: Trip capture from email or screenshot
nodes.source_file: ERA Notes/10 - Project Management/Trips/FABLED 2/4 - FABLED 2 — Future Enhancements.md
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: A PWA share target can turn booking confirmations into reviewed trip-date, destination, and template proposals.

nodes.label: Trips maturity snapshot
nodes.author: Elio
nodes.rationale: Overall maturity is scored 2.8: good ledger design, but near-zero verification, recoverability, cross-module safety, and tests.
